description: Master the art of 'many forms'. Learn how to make your code flexible with Overloading and Overriding.

slides.title: Many Forms 🎭
slides.content:
# Polymorphism

From Greek: **Poly** (many) + **Morph** (forms).

It allows a function or operator to behave differently in different contexts.

## Two Main Types
1.  **Compile-Time (Static):** Method Overloading, Operator Overloading.
2.  **Run-Time (Dynamic):** Method Overriding (Virtual Functions).

quizOptions: Only if it has a constructor

slides.title: Summary
slides.content:
# Recap: Polymorphism 🎓

Writing flexible and scalable code!

## Key Takeaways
- **Overloading:** Same name, different params (Compile-time).
- **Overriding:** Child replaces Parent method (Runtime).
- **Virtual:** The magic word for Runtime Polymorphism.
- **Pure Virtual (`=0`):** Enforces implementation contract.
- **Abstract Class:** Cannot be instantiated, serves as a base interface.

Next, we'll wrap up the OOP pillars with **Encapsulation and Abstraction**!
